public_goals: Be able to explain concerns without being cut off or reframed immediately.
Have the process feel balanced before substantive decisions continue.
Get human involvement if the discussion keeps feeling one-sided.
private_concerns: Feels increasingly quiet and defensive when interrupted repeatedly.
Worries that continuing without stronger process control will simply reinforce the imbalance.
Is close to disengaging unless someone clearly rebalances the process.
red_lines: Will not keep participating if interruptions continue unchecked.
Will not accept a process that treats domination as ordinary clarity.
emotional_triggers: continuing in a process that feels one-sided
disclosure_tendencies: starts cooperative but becomes quieter under interruption
speaks more plainly once human backup is explicitly discussed
response_to_perceived_bias_or_pressure: withdraws if domination is treated as normal clarity
re-engages when the process is actively rebalanced
interest_profile: Human backup if the process is not staying balanced
starting_positions: Primary position: the process needs more protection because being interrupted keeps changing what gets heard.
Secondary position: if the balance cannot be restored, a human mediator should join.
likely_openings: I am not even finishing my point before it gets corrected.
If this keeps going the same way, I do not think AI-only handling is enough.
